name: First Contact Physiotherapist
description: Our First Contact Physiotherapist is based in the practice and can assess and treat musculoskeletal problems — back pain, joint pain, and injuries — without a GP appointment.
howToRefer: Ask at reception to book directly. This is not the same as Community Physiotherapy (NHS Fife).

name: Podiatry / Foot Health
description: For conditions affecting your feet and lower limbs, including nail problems, skin conditions, and diabetes-related foot care.
howToRefer: Self-refer online via the NHS Fife Foot Care portal, or ask at reception. Advice line (not for appointments): 01383 518 635. Existing patients can contact Randolph Wemyss Hospital directly.

name: Pelvic Health Physiotherapy
description: Specialist physiotherapy for bladder, bowel, and pelvic floor problems — including incontinence, prolapse, and pelvic pain. Note: you can no longer self-refer for this service.
howToRefer: Ask your GP for a referral. A self-management leaflet is available from reception.

name: Virtual Hand Clinic
description: Assessment and advice for hand and wrist conditions — including fractures, tendon problems, and trigger finger — without a GP appointment.
howToRefer: Refer yourself via the Fife Virtual Hand Clinic website.

name: Optometry (eye health)
description: For red or sticky eye, pain around the eye, blurred vision, flashes and floaters, and headaches — contact your optometrist first. They can examine your eyes and refer you if needed.
howToRefer: Book directly with any NHS-registered optician. Visit Eyes.Scot for guidance on common eye conditions.

name: Foot Care Fife
description: A voluntary toe nail cutting service for eligible patients who are unable to manage this themselves.
howToRefer: Call the free helpline to check eligibility.

name: Access Therapies Fife
description: Free NHS talking therapies for anxiety, low mood, stress, and depression — including CBT, counselling, and guided self-help. No GP referral needed.
howToRefer: Self-refer online at accesstherapiesfife.scot.nhs.uk, call 01383 565 427, or email fife-uhbatfife@nhs.net. Webchat also available.

name: Practice Mental Health Nurses
description: Seonad and Andrew are our practice-based mental health nurses. They provide assessment and support for patients with mental health concerns.
howToRefer: Book direct via reception — no GP referral needed.

name: NHS 24 Mental Health Hub
description: Available 24 hours a day, 7 days a week. Call NHS 24 and select the mental health option to be connected to psychological wellbeing practitioners.
howToRefer: Call 111 and choose the mental health option.

name: Breathing Space
description: A free, confidential Scottish mental health helpline for anyone feeling low, anxious, or depressed.
howToRefer: Call 0800 83 85 87. Mon–Thu 6pm–2am, Fri 6pm–Mon 6am.

name: Sexual Health and Contraceptive Services
description: Confidential sexual health services including STI testing, contraception advice and fitting (including implants and coils), and treatment. No GP referral needed.
howToRefer: Call the clinic or book online at nashonlinebooking.com. Phone lines Mon–Fri 8:30am–11am and 1pm–4pm, plus Mon/Tue/Thu 5pm–7pm.

name: Maternity Services
description: If you think you might be pregnant and would like to book antenatal care, contact NHS Fife Maternity Services directly.
howToRefer: Complete the online self-referral at nhsfife.org/pregnancy or contact the antenatal clinic. Stop smoking in pregnancy: 01592 648 124 or 0800 025 3000.

name: Termination of Pregnancy
description: Confidential advice and referral for termination of pregnancy. You do not need to see a GP first.
howToRefer: Email fife.womenschoices@nhs.scot (staff will call back next working day). Or call 01592 643355 ext 25445, Mon–Fri 12pm–4pm.

name: Sexual Assault Support (SARCS)
description: The Sexual Assault Referral Centre (SARCS) provides confidential support for anyone who has experienced sexual assault — recent or historical. Available 24 hours a day.
howToRefer: Call the 24-hour self-referral line. No appointment or GP referral needed.

name: Fife Childhood Immunisation Service
description: For queries about childhood vaccinations, schedules, or missed doses — contact the NHS Fife Childhood Immunisation Service directly.
howToRefer: Call Mon–Fri 9am–5pm or email. No GP referral needed.

name: Fife School Nursing Service
description: Health support for school-age children. School nurses run health drop-in zones in high schools across Fife.
howToRefer: Contact via school or call the school nursing service.

name: Travel Health and Vaccinations
description: Please note: Scoonie Medical Practice no longer provides travel health services or travel vaccinations. For travel health and vaccinations, contact NHS Fife or use a registered travel health clinic. For general travel health information, visit travelhealthpro.org.uk.
howToRefer: Visit the NHS Fife travel vaccination service page or contact a local pharmacy or travel clinic.

name: Emergency Dental Care
description: For urgent dental problems, please do not come to the health centre — we are unable to treat dental conditions. Contact your dentist first. If you don't have one or they're closed, call the Fife Dental Help Line.
howToRefer: Call the Fife Dental Help Line for urgent dental advice and out-of-hours access. Or call NHS 24 on 111.

name: Stop Smoking (Quit Your Way)
description: Free NHS support to stop smoking — including one-to-one advice, nicotine replacement therapy, and group sessions.
howToRefer: Email fife.smokingcessation@nhs.scot or call the free helpline. Your pharmacist can also help.

name: Working Health Services
description: Free, confidential support for employees of businesses with fewer than 250 staff, whose health is affecting their ability to work. Rapid access to physiotherapy, talking therapies, and occupational health.
howToRefer: Call to self-refer. Available to anyone currently in employment, including part-time and self-employed workers.

name: Bladder and Bowel Service (BABS)
description: Specialist NHS support for bladder and bowel problems, including incontinence, constipation, and urgency.
howToRefer: Email fife.communitycontinence@nhs.scot or call.

name: Social Work (Fife Council)
description: For community care, adult support, carer assessments, and social work advice.
howToRefer: Call the Fife Council Social Work contact centre directly. No GP referral needed.

name: Fife Bus (community transport)
description: Accessible minibus transport for patients with reduced mobility (physical or mental, temporary or permanent) who have no other way to get to medical appointments.
howToRefer: Call to check eligibility and book transport.

name: RVS East Fife Community Transport
description: Voluntary transport service helping older and disabled people get to medical and other essential appointments.
howToRefer: Email or call to arrange transport. A small contribution may be requested.

name: Minor Injuries Unit
description: For non-life-threatening but painful injuries — cuts, minor burns, sprains, suspected broken bones, and animal bites. Call NHS 111 before travelling to check your nearest open unit.
howToRefer: Phone 111 for a Minor Injuries Unit appointment and to get directions to your nearest unit.
